inngest.name: Inngest
inngest.short_description: Inngest is a durable execution platform for building reliable workflows, AI agents, and background jobs directly in application code.

features.description: Steps retry independently from their failure point without restarting the entire run.
features.title: Durable execution

features.description: Rate limiting, per-tenant concurrency limits, and throttling for workflow execution.
features.title: Flow control

features.description: Step-level tracing, replay functionality, and evaluation metrics for AI agents and workflows.
features.title: Agent observability

features.description: Embeds into existing applications via an SDK supporting Next.js, Node.js, and Python.
features.title: SDK-based integration

features.description: SOC 2 Type II compliant, with a HIPAA BAA available for regulated industries.
features.title: Compliance

pros: Free Hobby plan with no credit card required
Embeds directly into existing application code without new infrastructure
Step-level tracing, replay, and evaluation for debugging workflows and AI agents
SOC 2 Type II compliant with HIPAA BAA available
Used by companies including Replit, ElevenLabs, Cohere, SoundCloud, and TripAdvisor
cons: Pro plan pricing scales with usage (executions, concurrency, data ingested), which can raise costs at higher scale
Enterprise-only features like SAML, RBAC, and 90-day trace retention require contacting sales
SDK-based approach means it's built for developers working in code rather than a visual/no-code interface

faqs.answer: Yes, Inngest has a free Hobby plan with no credit card required, including 50k executions and 500k events ingested per month.
faqs.question: Is Inngest free?

faqs.answer: The Pro plan starts at $99/month and includes 1M+ executions and 100+ concurrent executions.
faqs.question: How much does Inngest's Pro plan cost?

faqs.answer: Inngest released its durable execution platform in 2022.
faqs.question: When was Inngest founded?

faqs.answer: Inngest is headquartered in San Francisco, with a remote-first team.
faqs.question: Where is Inngest based?

features: Granular tracing, metrics, alerts + 7 day trace retention

mockoon.name: Mockoon
mockoon.short_description: Mockoon is a free, open-source desktop app for designing and running mock REST APIs locally, with an optional paid Cloud tier.

features.description: Design and run mock REST APIs in seconds through an intuitive desktop interface, with no remote deployment or account needed.
features.title: API Mocking

features.description: Simulate realistic scenarios and test edge cases using templating and rule-based response matching.
features.title: Dynamic Templating & Rules Matching

features.description: Capture real HTTP requests from a live API and replay them to quickly build mocks.
features.title: Requests Recording

features.description: Forward unmocked requests through to a real API while mocking only selected endpoints.
features.title: Proxy Mode

features.description: Import and export OpenAPI specifications to fit existing API design workflows.
features.title: OpenAPI Compatibility

features.description: Run mocks headlessly on servers, in Docker containers, or serverless platforms (AWS Lambda, GCP Functions, Firebase Functions), and in CI/CD (GitHub Actions, CircleCI, TravisCI).
features.title: CLI & Docker

features.description: Deploy mocks to the cloud and collaborate with a team in real time (paid Cloud tier).
features.title: Mockoon Cloud Collaboration

pros: Free, open-source desktop app with no account required
Supports Windows, macOS, and Linux
CLI and Docker support for CI/CD and headless environments
OpenAPI import/export fits existing API design workflows
Active, offline-first, privacy-friendly project maintained since 2017
cons: Real-time team collaboration and cloud deployment require a paid Mockoon Cloud subscription
Team plan is priced at $100/month billed annually rather than offering a lower-cost individual paid tier
Enterprise pricing is custom/quote-only, requiring direct contact

faqs.answer: Yes, the desktop application, CLI, and Docker image are open-source and free to use with no account required.
faqs.question: Is Mockoon free?

faqs.answer: A paid add-on offering cloud-hosted mock deployments and real-time team collaboration, with the Team plan starting at $100/month billed annually.
faqs.question: What is Mockoon Cloud?

faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial is available (no card needed for work emails; 7 days with a card for others).
faqs.question: Does Mockoon Cloud offer a free trial?

faqs.answer: Windows, macOS, and Linux desktop installs, plus CLI and Docker for servers and CI/CD.
faqs.question: What platforms does Mockoon support?

faqs.answer: Yes, Mockoon is compatible with OpenAPI for importing and exporting API designs.
faqs.question: Can Mockoon import OpenAPI specs?

features: Free desktop app for Windows, macOS, and Linux
CLI and Docker image for headless/self-hosted use
Full core mocking, templating, and proxy features
3 API mocks deployed in the cloud with real-time collaboration
200 AI-generated endpoints per month per user
Enterprise support with a customer success manager
Custom payment options (PO, invoicing, bank transfer)

wiremock.name: WireMock
wiremock.short_description: WireMock is an open-source HTTP API simulation tool, with an optional hosted WireMock Cloud tier for teams and enterprises.

features.description: Matches requests on URL patterns, headers, query parameters, cookies, and request body.
features.title: Request matching

features.description: Generates dynamic responses using Handlebars templating.
features.title: Dynamic response templating

features.description: Records real API traffic and replays it for realistic test scenarios.
features.title: Record and playback

features.description: Simulates network delays and malformed responses to test edge cases.
features.title: Fault simulation

features.description: Verifies that expected requests were made during test runs.
features.title: Request verification

features.description: Runs as a standalone JAR, embedded in tests, or as a Docker container.
features.title: Flexible deployment

features.description: A commercial hosted platform adding stateful mocking, database import, OpenAPI sync, and UI-based mock creation with AI support.
features.title: WireMock Cloud (hosted)

pros: Free and open source with no cost for the core simulation engine
Supports fault simulation, request verification, and Handlebars-based dynamic responses
Flexible deployment as a standalone JAR, embedded library, or Docker container
Broad framework support including Java (Maven, Gradle, Kotlin, Scala SBT) and .NET via WireMock.Net
WireMock Cloud offers a free plan with no credit card required
cons: WireMock Cloud's free plan is limited to 1,000 API calls/month, one user, and 3 mock APIs
WireMock Cloud Enterprise pricing is not publicly listed and requires contacting sales
Stateful mocking, team collaboration, and SSO/RBAC require the paid Enterprise tier
Free plan on WireMock Cloud caps request rate at 10 requests per second

faqs.answer: Yes, the core WireMock tool is free and open source. WireMock Cloud, the hosted commercial platform, also has a free plan with usage limits.
faqs.question: Is WireMock free to use?

faqs.answer: Yes, WireMock Cloud is a commercial hosted platform with a free tier (1,000 calls/month, 1 user, 3 mock APIs) and a custom-priced Enterprise tier.
faqs.question: Does WireMock have a hosted cloud offering?

faqs.answer: WireMock supports Java (via Maven, Gradle for Groovy/Kotlin) and Scala SBT, plus .NET via the WireMock.Net implementation, and can run standalone or in Docker.
faqs.question: What languages and frameworks does WireMock support?

faqs.answer: Yes, WireMock Cloud offers UI-based mock creation with AI support.
faqs.question: Does WireMock Cloud include AI features?

groundcover.name: Groundcover
groundcover.short_description: Cloud-native observability platform using eBPF for zero-instrumentation data collection, deployed inside the customer's own cloud (BYOC).

features.description: Uses eBPF sensors to gather telemetry across infrastructure, applications, and AI workloads without code changes.
features.title: Zero-instrumentation data collection

features.description: Deploys entirely within the customer's own cloud VPC for data privacy and control.
features.title: BYOC architecture

features.description: Combines logs, traces, metrics, events, and real user monitoring in one platform.

features.description: Dedicated monitoring for LLM calls and agent pipelines.

features.description: An AI agent that investigates issues and can execute remediation tasks.

features.description: Automated tools to transition dashboards and monitors from legacy observability platforms.
features.title: Migration tools

pros: Free forever tier with no credit card required
BYOC deployment keeps all telemetry data inside the customer's own cloud account
Per-host pricing model avoids ingestion-based cost surprises
eBPF-based collection requires no manual code instrumentation
On Premise option available for fully self-hosted data plane and UI
cons: Free plan is limited to 12-hour data retention
Enterprise and On Premise tiers cost more per host ($35-$50/host/month)
Pricing is based on Kubernetes node counts, which requires a Kubernetes-based environment

faqs.answer: Yes, a free forever tier is available with 12-hour data retention and no credit card required.
faqs.question: Does groundcover have a free plan?

faqs.answer: Pricing is based on the monthly average number of Kubernetes nodes monitored, starting at $30/host/month for the Pro plan.
faqs.question: How is groundcover priced?

faqs.answer: BYOC (Bring Your Own Cloud) means groundcover deploys entirely within the customer's own cloud environment rather than as a third-party SaaS.

faqs.answer: groundcover is headquartered in Tel Aviv, Israel, with an additional presence in California.

openobserve.name: OpenObserve
openobserve.short_description: OpenObserve is an open-source observability platform unifying logs, metrics, traces, and RUM with low-cost object storage.

features.description: Combines logs, metrics, traces, dashboards, alerts, and real user monitoring in one platform.

features.description: Ingests data via OpenTelemetry with no proprietary vendor lock-in.

pros: Free, fully open-source OSS edition with no usage limits
Self-hosted Enterprise tier is free up to 50 GB/day ingestion
Lower storage costs than Elasticsearch via columnar compression
14-day free trial on Cloud plans with no credit card required
SOC 2 Type II and ISO 27001 certified on the Cloud offering
cons: Cloud pricing is usage-based per GB, which can be harder to predict at scale
Advanced AI and enterprise capabilities require a paid or Enterprise plan
Self-hosted Enterprise features beyond 50 GB/day require contacting sales

faqs.answer: Yes, the self-hosted OSS edition is free with unlimited usage, and Cloud plans include a 14-day free trial.

faqs.answer: Logs, metrics, traces, RUM, and session replay in one unified platform.

faqs.answer: Cloud plans charge $0.50/GB for ingestion and $0.01/GB for queries, with a 30% discount for annual commitment.

faqs.answer: Self-hosted Enterprise is free up to 50 GB/day of ingestion; above that requires contacting sales.

faqs.answer: Yes, it's licensed under AGPL-3.0 with more than 20,000 GitHub stars.
